findings.message: The post_item endpoint persists user-supplied input through the bare create_item(), bypassing create_item_normalized() — the documented canonical entry-point for user input. Both repository docstrings state that callers accepting user input MUST go through create_item_normalized (strip + lowercase). Routing this user-facing write through create_item breaks the data-store's normalization invariant: names enter the items table with arbitrary casing and surrounding whitespace, diverging from every name written via the canonical path. The data flow (HTTP payload -> ItemCreate.name -> create_item -> Item.name) has no consistency strategy, so the same logical name (e.g. ' Apple ' vs 'apple') is stored as distinct values, corrupting downstream reads, lookups, and any uniqueness assumptions.
findings.suggestion: Call create_item_normalized(session, payload.name) instead of create_item, so the user-supplied name follows the documented strip+lowercase normalization before it reaches the store.
